key_issues: J1 (Major): Resource-subscription update notifications have no owner. Client has subscribe/unsubscribe (M13) but no module emits 'notifications/resources/updated' or tracks subscriptions server-side (M12). Half-wired primitive; fails Success Criterion 9.4.
J2 (Major): List pagination (cursors) is unaddressed. No M12/M13 interface mentions producing/following nextCursor on */list operations; breaks wire-protocol parity (G1) and TS-client interop (G2).

J3 (Major): JSON-RPC batch is declared as a type in M1 but no module assembles/disassembles batches; engine (M11) describes only single request/response correlation. Internally inconsistent - resolve per target spec revision (engine handles batch, or M1 drops the envelope).
N1 (Minor): Versioned-spec normalization facade asserted but strategy (normalized superset vs version-tagged variants) unspecified; matters for how handlers see 2025-11-25 vs 2026-07-28 differences.

N2 (Minor): SSE resumption-token mint/validate interface obligation underspecified (storage correctly deferred, but the interface responsibility on M6/M8 is only gestured at) despite resumable streams being a hard Reliability NFR.
N3 (Minor/cosmetic): M12 bundles low-level Server, high-level McpServer, and Completable under one module number, asymmetric with M5's sub-lettering; completions easy to overlook in coverage trace.

criteria_verification.all_vision_requirements_covered: mostly - feature-level coverage complete via 3.3/3.4; gaps at primitive sub-feature level (J1 resource-updated, J2 pagination, J3 batch)
criteria_verification.interfaces_sufficient_granularity: yes - internal vs external separated per module with responsibilities; 3.3 summarizes comms; gaps are missing responsibilities on defined interfaces, not missing interfaces
criteria_verification.no_implementation_details_enforced: yes - explicitly disclaimed (line 9) and held to; naming illustrative, deferred decisions parked in section 5; strongest dimension
criteria_verification.tech_stack_consistent: yes - architecture section 4 matches vision 5.1 row-for-row; uniform green-threads/contracts/std-lib stance; no unsanctioned tech
